title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a safe tool-use decision protocol after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.
Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Tool Description evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Tool Description assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Tool Description, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a safe tool-use decision protocol.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: The tool name alone is enough for reliable selection. This fails the Tool Description contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: Anthropic Prompt Engineering Overview (Current 2026-07-13) — Controls success criteria, empirical evaluation, and the living Claude prompt-engineering workflow.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Write tool descriptions and schemas that make capability, inputs, outputs, and side effects unambiguous.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: The tool name alone is enough for reliable selection.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

stimulus.title: Tool Failure Recovery case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Repeating the identical failing tool call is a robust recovery strategy.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly. Artifact under review: a safe tool-use decision protocol.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Tool Failure Recovery, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Tool Failure Recovery applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle timeouts, partial results, retries, and alternate evidence without looping blindly.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
